headline: Botswana first-baseline rotation assessment with material in-window positive evidence. Published score 60.9 (Established). UNAIDS confirmed April 30 2026 that Botswana fully repealed colonial-era anti-LGBTQ+ law: Penal Code amended to delete 'Unnatural Offences' provisions that previously criminalized same-sex relationships with up to seven years' imprisonment. This is structural rights protection following 2019 High Court ruling and 2021 Court of Appeal upholding. President Duma Boko (human-rights lawyer who litigated LEGABIBO and Kanane LGBTQI cases) elected late 2024; pledged to redistribute diamond wealth and reduce overreliance on diamonds. Material EQU and BND uplift; band Established sustained but at higher composite. Composite 67.5 vs published 60.9 — +6.6 delta is MATERIAL CHANGE; flag-for-review under upgrade pathway.
key_evidence: April 28-30 2026 (in window): Botswana formally repealed colonial-era 'Unnatural Offences' Penal Code provisions criminalizing same-sex relationships with up to 7 years' imprisonment. UNAIDS publicly applauded the repeal.
Repeal followed 2019 High Court ruling (provisions unconstitutional) upheld by Court of Appeal in 2021 — this is the legislative codification of rights established by judicial review.
President Duma Boko elected late 2024 — human rights lawyer, previously litigated Kanane and LEGABIBO LGBTQI registration cases on behalf of plaintiffs.
Boko pledged to redistribute diamond wealth and reduce overreliance on diamonds (80% of exports).
Anti-corruption posture: Boko explicitly campaigned against BDP's corruption history.
LEGABIBO active LGBTQ+ rights advocacy organization in country.

evidence.finding: UNAIDS applauds Botswana on fully repealing anti-LGBTQ+ law.
Botswana repeals colonial-era sodomy law; legislative codification follows judicial review.
Decriminalization of same-sex relations: national, regional, and international LGBTQI+ activism dimensions; Botswana as regional leader.
Boko: human rights lawyer, mission orientation; pledged diamond-wealth redistribution and economic diversification.
Boko background: human rights and constitutional lawyer; emerged as social-justice and humane-governance leader.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.6 (+0.1). Modest uplift via active LGBTQI+ advocacy organization (LEGABIBO) and government engagement.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.6 (+0.1). Boko's litigation history on minority-rights cases reflects E2 Perspective-Taking embedded in leadership.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.7 (+0.2). AC1 Responsiveness: legislative repeal of unconstitutional provisions is a documented action, not just stated commitment. Anchor moves toward 4.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.7 (+0.7). EQ1 Universality: removal of criminal provisions from Penal Code is a major universality improvement. EQ5 Historical Harm Acknowledgment: codifying colonial-era harm reversal. Anchor moves substantially toward 4.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.8 (+0.3). B5 Consent Orientation and B3 Scope Clarity: Botswana's posture aligns law with judicial review and international human-rights norms. Anchor moves toward 4.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.7 (+0.2). AB2 Correction Willingness: legislative correction of Penal Code following judicial finding is documented institutional learning.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.7 (+0.2). S4 Structural Critique: structural decriminalization aligns with regional/international framework; Botswana's leadership in southern Africa noted.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.8 (+0.3). I2 Non-Performance: Boko's pre-presidency LGBTQI+ litigation work is publicly documented and continues in office. I4 Values Alignment: legislative codification of campaign commitments. I1 Consistency: human-rights lawyer continuing human-rights orientation in office.

math_check.validator_check: +6.6 MATERIAL change above 5pt threshold. EQU dimension change is +0.7 — within validator 2.0pt cycle ceiling. The EQU shift is the dominant driver and is justified by the structural decriminalization event.

negativeSignals: Diamond-economy concentration (80% of exports); economic diversification still in early stages; anti-corruption commitments not yet stress-tested.
positiveSignals: In-window legislative repeal of colonial-era anti-LGBTQ+ Penal Code provisions; UNAIDS public recognition; President Boko's pre-presidency human-rights litigation history; constitutional codification of rights established by judicial review.
sector_context: Botswana's repeal positions it as a regional leader in southern African LGBTQI+ rights, distinct from neighboring countries with more restrictive frameworks. This is one of the strongest in-window positive structural-rights signals across all assessed countries this cycle.
open_actions_resolved: April 28-30 anti-LGBTQ+ Penal Code repeal scored as material EQU/BND/INT positive evidence
Boko human-rights leadership profile captured under INT
open_actions_carried: Monitor implementation of Penal Code amendments
Monitor diamond-economy diversification commitments under SYS dimension
Re-queue for full assessment at second rotation cycle to confirm trajectory
